what_it_does: A grounding/confirmation result for the A2-rhm-mobile-elevated.html mockup (the mobile RHM chat interface with a glowing voice orb). Holds 17 candidate dossiers reduced to 11 clusters, each with a ui:// address, capabilities, grounding flag, the what/what-you-can-do/how-to-change triple, a maps_to_feature id, and a 3-draw jury verdict sorting them into confirmed (3) and flagged (8).
observations: ground block describes RHM as a living conversational entity with a breathing orb, listening ring, record, and config controls.
Confirmed: chat mode selector (RHM-modes), the memo-gate inquiry (proposed), and the 'not now' decline button (RHM-verbs, unanimous).
How-to text repeatedly cites RhmChat.tsx as the surface and suite.py:chat as the brain/logic.
Refcheck fabrications flagged: RHM-identity and RHM-utterance (appearing on multiple dossiers).
Two distinct dossiers share the address ui://chat/utterance with different capabilities (presentable vs driven) and different represented meanings.
A recurring narrative claim: 'your turns train the twin, its working-grade inference drives the system' (chat/rhm dossier).
standouts: The 'twin training' framing: operator turns are cast as training data for a working-grade inference twin that then drives the system.
RHM-utterance is flagged as a fabricated feature id yet appears repeatedly as an intended mapping, signaling a feature the mockup wants but the inventory lacks.
Address collision: ui://chat/utterance used by two separate dossiers.
conventions: Same audit schema as the canvas siblings (ground / counts / confirmed / flagged, howto triple, verdict object).
ui://chat/<element> addressing for the RHM surface.
concerns: Duplicate ui://chat/utterance address across two dossiers risks ambiguous resolution.
RHM-utterance and RHM-identity repeatedly fail refcheck as non-inventory ids, suggesting the mockup outran the feature inventory.
Several 'built'-grounded controls (chat/run, chat/toolbar, chat/rhm) get 0–1 of 3 jury grounding, an internal built-vs-jury tension.
notes: The mobile RHM-chat slice of the UI-grounding audit; surfaces a voice/twin conversational entity and repeatedly flags RHM-utterance/RHM-identity as feature ids missing from the inventory.
questions: Should RHM-utterance and RHM-identity be added to the feature inventory, or remapped to existing ids?
How is the duplicate ui://chat/utterance address meant to be disambiguated by consumers?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: an honest grounding audit of the RHM mobile chat mockup elements.
apparent_themes: Conversational/voice presence (RHM) as a living entity
intention_signals: Aims to ground the RHM voice-chat surface honestly — confirming code-backed controls (modes, decline) and flagging the aspirational RHM-utterance/identity features that the mockup imagines but the inventory does not yet hold.
novelty: The RHM 'twin' conversational framing and the cluster of aspirational RHM-* feature ids that fail refcheck.
contribution: Contributes the RHM-mobile grounding-audit record: the voice/chat element inventory, the twin-training narrative, and the confirmed/flagged verdicts a fused system would keep as the RHM-mobile grounding evidence.
summary_for_embedding: A UI-grounding audit result for the A2 RHM mobile chat mockup (voice orb interface), clustering 17 element dossiers into 3 confirmed and 8 flagged via refcheck and a 3-draw soft jury.
intention_for_embedding: To verify each RHM mobile chat element is truthfully grounded in code and flag aspirational RHM feature ids absent from the inventory for operator review.
